name: Fukuoka / Itoshima
panel_verdict: Keep as a top-tier shortlist candidate. It is the “highest probability of working” option rather than the most romantic one.

name: Valencia
panel_verdict: Keep near the top. Best suited for retirement optionality and long-stay demand, not ultra-luxury holiday yield.

name: Algarve / Cascais
panel_verdict: Keep as a core European benchmark. Strong for retirement and lifestyle, only average for development yield.

name: Málaga / Costa del Sol
panel_verdict: Keep, but require strict entry-price discipline. Good destination; not necessarily good at any price.

name: Lake Como
panel_verdict: Keep for prestige and long-term liquidity. Do not rank it as a yield destination unless a very specific asset is mispriced.

name: Hakone / Izu
panel_verdict: Keep as a practical Japan alternative. Best for personal use and domestic demand, less compelling as a global trophy asset.

name: Madeira
panel_verdict: Keep as a differentiated Europe water/nature candidate. Attractive, but size positions conservatively because liquidity is thinner.

name: Costa Brava / Girona
panel_verdict: Keep. One of the better “quality of life plus accessible coast” European options.

name: Hakuba
panel_verdict: Keep as an upside candidate. It is more venture-like than Fukuoka or Algarve: higher upside, higher operating risk.

name: Crete
panel_verdict: Keep as a value-oriented Europe candidate. Strong for lifestyle, but underwrite conservatively for exit and operations.

name: Annecy
panel_verdict: Keep as a premium lifestyle contender. Excellent to own; hard to make the numbers exciting.

name: Mallorca
panel_verdict: Keep, but only if licence and micro-location are exceptional. It is a mature market, not a bargain market.

name: Niseko
panel_verdict: Keep as a trophy/specialist candidate, not a default top pick. Needs asset-specific edge to justify the price.

name: Croatia / Istria-Dalmatia
panel_verdict: Watchlist. Attractive value, but needs sharper local partner and legal diligence than Spain/Portugal.

name: Queenstown
panel_verdict: Specialist only. Beautiful and high quality, but ownership constraints make it hard to rank near the top.

name: Phuket / Koh Samui
net_yield_estimate: 3–6% est. net; leasehold risk should require higher hurdle rate
panel_verdict: Keep as a yield candidate only with conservative legal structuring. Not a clean core holding.

name: Dolomites / South Tyrol
panel_verdict: Keep as a premium mountain benchmark. Great lifestyle asset, but financial return depends on buying unusually well.

name: Bali
net_yield_estimate: 3.5–6.5% est. net; only attractive with strong operator and conservative lease math
panel_verdict: Specialist/yield bucket. Worth studying, but only with excellent legal/operator control and higher required return.

name: Chamonix
panel_verdict: Keep as a benchmark, not a priority acquisition unless the asset is exceptional.

name: Da Nang / Hoi An
panel_verdict: Watchlist. Cheap is not enough; only proceed with a very clean title/project and strong operator.

name: Whistler
panel_verdict: Watchlist/specialist. Excellent destination, poor fit unless legal eligibility and price are unusually favourable.

name: Innsbruck / Tyrol
panel_verdict: Keep as a lifestyle benchmark, not a leading development candidate unless a legally rentable asset is identified.

name: Andermatt
panel_verdict: Best Swiss option for a foreign buyer, but more capital-preservation/lifestyle than profit-maximisation.

name: Ticino / Lake Lugano
panel_verdict: Specialist retirement/lifestyle candidate. Good to live in, less compelling as a development investment.

name: Swiss Valais / Vaud Alps
panel_verdict: Do not prioritise for this mandate. Keep only as a luxury benchmark or if personal-use value dominates return objectives.
